content.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
content.summary:
Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths

Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian.

The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover

content.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
content.summary:
Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates

There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial.

It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists

content.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
content.summary:
Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say

Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official.

Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a stat

content.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
content.summary:
Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans

Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%.

More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Sa

content.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
content.summary:
President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption

A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January.

The fake agency came to l

content.title: Mexico to file criminal complaints over migrants killed by ICE in US
content.summary:
Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexicans ‘outraged’ over killing last week of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by agents in Houston

Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Monday that Mexico would be filing criminal complaints in the US for the deaths of more than a dozen Mexican migrants in immigration detention and those killed in anti-migrant operations.

The deaths include last week’s <

content.title: Experts warn of ways screwworm could spread in the US and new difficulties in keeping it at bay
content.summary:
Scientists worry that current eradication efforts won’t be able to contain parasitic infestation pushing into US

When conservationists set up cameras in remote regions of Central American forests, they wanted to monitor illegal cattle movement, which can lead to deforestation. But in recent months, they discovered another alarming development: wildlife rapidly infected with the new world screwworm.

It’s a warning

content.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
content.summary:
Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-prepared

Even before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support.

Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed ap

content.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
content.summary:
Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families

A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other.

Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the sh

content.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
content.summary:
Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help

The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday.

At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people a

content.title: Pakistan urges restraint as its Mideast deal unravels
content.summary: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.

content.title: People watching the World Cup during work hours is leading to a drop in productivity
content.summary: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.

content.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
content.summary: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

content.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
content.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

content.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
content.summary: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

content.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
content.summary: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.

content.title: Investigators probe a fire at a music bar in Bangkok that killed at least 27 people
content.summary: By Monday morning, forensic teams were investigating the scene. Officials say many victims were trapped in windowless bathrooms near a rear exit. Investigators are focusing on the ceiling above the performance stage for clues.

content.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
content.summary: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.

content.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
content.summary: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

content.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran and Tehran attacks U.S. allies in the Gulf
content.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.

content.title: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and vows 20% charge on cargo passing through Hormuz
content.summary: The US military later said it had started the third consecutive night of strikes on Iran.

content.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
content.summary: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

content.title: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
content.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

content.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar
content.summary: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

content.title: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78
content.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

content.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
content.summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.

content.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
content.summary: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

content.title: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
content.summary: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".

content.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
content.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

content.title: Sudan's paramilitary RSF chief sentenced to death over war crimes
content.summary: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo was convicted in absentia along with 15 RSF commanders and allies.

content.title: Haaland’s Norway receive heroes’ welcome in Oslo after World Cup exit
content.summary: A crowd of more than 100,000, an open-top bus parade and a royal reception welcomed back the Norwegian World Cup squad.

content.title: Police in South Africa open probe into post-World Cup death of Jayden Adams
content.summary: The country's football players' union confirmed Adams's death on Saturday, but the cause has not been revealed.

content.title: US, Iran exchange attacks around Strait of Hormuz
content.summary: This is a breaking news story.

content.title: Lindsey Graham’s legacy: Israel advocate, Trump ally, Iran war supporter
content.summary: Late US senator backed invasion of Iraq, war on Iran and approach of 'more for Israel' as he became a Trump confidant.

content.title: How is the US tech industry regulated?
content.summary: Apple files a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing it of stealing trade secrets.

content.title: Andy Burnham in line to become British PM after securing party support
content.summary: Burnham's bid cannot be challenged by fellow MPs, as he now has the support of almost the entire parliamentary party.

content.title: Argentina vs England World Cup tickets more expensive than France vs Spain
content.summary: Prices for the Argentina vs England semifinal have seen a 34 percent increase over the past three days.

content.title: US strikes Iranian ship with drone boats in first-of-its-kind attack
content.summary: US CENTCOM has published a video it says shows three Corsair unmanned surface vessels striking an Iranian ship facility

content.title: Kyiv attacked after Ukraine launches coalition to tackle Russia missiles
content.summary: Russia has escalated attacks in recent weeks, taking advantage of Ukraine’s critical shortage of air defences.

content.title: Iran military warns US against Strait of Hormuz ‘interference’
content.summary: Iran's military has warned it will not allow the United States to ‘interfere’ in the management of the Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: Trump, 'un accordo con l'Iran è ancora possibile'
content.summary: 'Teheran non avrà l'arma nucleare'.

content.title: Trump, 'vogliamo essere rimborsati per lo stretto di Hormuz'
content.summary: 'Stiamo proteggendo un'area molto ricca'

content.title: Trump, 'attaccheremo tutte le capacità dell'Iran nello stretto di Hormuz'
content.summary: 'Il nuovo conflitto sarà molto veloce e finirà presto'

content.summary: La missione della Fab punta a contrastare la carenza di beni essenziali sull'isola

content.summary: Félix Plasencia nominato alla guida del nuovo dicastero per la politica internazionale

content.summary: 'Colpiti anche alcuni siti militari strategici dell'esercito Usa In Kuwait'

content.title: Messico, chiesto l'ergastolo per lo storico leader del cartello di Sinaloa El Mayo
content.summary: La procura Usa accusa il leader del cartello di Sinaloa di narcotraffico e corruzione

content.title: Trump, 'presto bombarderemo in Iran l'impianto nucleare di Pickaxe Mountain'
content.summary: E' un complesso sotterraneo di tunnel di un sito segreto vicino a Natanz

content.title: US judge voids Donald Trump 'improper' $1.8 billion settlement with IRS
content.summary: A US judge on Monday voided a settlement between President Donald Trump and the Internal Revenue Service over leaked tax returns, ruling the lawsuit was filed for an "improper purpose." The agreement had granted Trump, his family and business broad protections from tax audits following their $10 billion lawsuit.

content.title: 'We will never eliminate the risk of fire completely', expert says as wildfire rages south of Paris
content.summary: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Monte Francis, Stefan Doerr, Director of the Centre for Wildfire Research at Swansea University, says that "we need to prepare the environment more to reduce the risk of fire but we will never eliminate it competely". France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris. Throughout the day, aircraft in the skies and firefighters on the ground were trying to douse the flames.

content.title: Ukraine strikes halt shipping on key wheat route as Russian fuel crisis deepens
content.summary: A daily update on the latest business and economic news.

content.title: Ebola spreads to more provinces
content.summary: In tonight's show, the Ebola outbreak spreads to two more provinces. A Sudanese court sentences paramilitary leader Mohamed Hamdan Daglo and 15 others to death in absentia. Residents of the Niger Delta town of Billé take to the streets to demand government action over catastrophic pollution.

content.title: Ukraine and allies set up coalition to tackle Russia ballistic missile threat
content.summary: Ukraine and key Western allies on Monday announced an ​air-defence coalition that would include jointly developing a new anti-ballistic missile system as an alternative and cheaper solution to the U.S. Patriot system. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports from Ukraine.

content.title: Nearly 1,000 hectares burnt in fire near Paris
content.summary: A fire raging in the historic and much-visited Fontainebleau forest south of Paris on Monday has prompted evacuations and disrupted train and highway traffic. Authorities say the fire, which has destroyed nearly five percent of the 25,000-hectare forest, could have been started by arson. It comes as the region is endures its third heatwave of the ​summer, with tinder-dry vegetation and ‌high temperatures fuelling blazes. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

content.title: Trump reimposes Iran naval blockade, threatens Hormuz fees
content.summary: President Donald Trump reimposed a US naval blockade on Iranian ports on Monday and vowed hefty fees for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, as Tehran mockingly pledged to charge a lower rate for passage through the strategic waterway. FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin speaks with Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace and Former State Department Middle East Analyst.

content.title: Two arrested as Fontainebleau forest near Paris ravaged by fire, prompting evacuations
content.summary: France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris on Monday, as police arrested two people suspected of starting it. The fire erupted Sunday in the sprawling Fontainebleau forest, about 60 kilometres (40 miles) southeast of the capital, a former royal hunting estate that today is dotted with quiet villages. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports.

content.title: Replay: Macron speaks as Ukraine’s ‘Coalition of the Willing’ meeting ends in Paris
content.summary: Ukraine has ​ordered 16 Rafale fighter jets, an initial batch of new-generation SAMP/T surface-to-air defence batteries, and licences for the production of ‌several capabilities, including the Scalp cruise missile, French President Emmanuel Macron said ⁠Monday after a meeting of more than 20 "Coalition of the Willing" leaders in Paris

content.title: 🔴 Ukraine orders 16 Rafale jets, announces new anti-ballistic missile coalition in Paris
Person killed in ICE-involved shooting in Maine
content.summary: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents were involved in a fatal shooting in Biddeford, according to Maine's House speaker. It comes days after ICE allegedly shot a man in Texas during a traffic stop.

content.title: South Korea airs concerns over North Korean border activity
content.summary: South Korea has voiced concern that its neighbor's actions along the demilitarized zone between the two countries may be in breach of the 1953 armistice agreement.

content.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
content.summary: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

content.title: Kenya: What impact will Dangote's new refinery have on the region?
content.summary: Is Kenya Africa's next major energy hub? A plan from Dangote Industries to build a massive refinery off the country's coast could reshape fuel supply, trade and economic growth across East Africa.

content.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
content.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

content.title: Renewed US-Iran war is hitting Gulf countries hard
content.summary: After US strikes on Iran, Iran has targeted US allies in the Middle East. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

content.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
content.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

content.title: Sam Neill, Jurassic Park actor, dies 'unexpectedly' at 78
content.summary: New Zealand actor Sam Neill achieved international fame playing Dr. Alan Grant in the 1993 film Jurassic Park. But his career included many other notable TV and film appearances.

content.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
content.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

content.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
content.summary: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

content.title: Lunar Gateway: Earth’s guard post against asteroids?
content.summary: Humankind’s next space outpost, the lunar Gateway, will serve as a staging point to reach the surface of the Moon. A new ESA-backed study is considering whether it could also be used as a deployment point for planetary defence missions, to intercept asteroids approaching dangerously close to Earth.

content.title: ESA commissions world’s first space debris removal
content.summary: ClearSpace-1 will be the first space mission to remove an item of debris from orbit, planned for launch in 2025. The mission is being procured as a service contract with a startup-led commercial consortium, to help establish a new market for in-orbit servicing, as well as debris removal.

content.title: Hera’s radar CubeSat will peer into asteroid’s heart
content.summary: ESA’s proposed Hera asteroid mission for planetary defence will carry the smallest radar instrument ever flown in space – flying on a separate mini-spacecraft, it will approach two asteroids closer than its mothership and probe deep into their cores.

content.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
content.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

content.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
content.summary: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.

content.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
content.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software wi

content.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
content.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

content.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
content.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

content.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
content.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

content.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
content.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

content.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
content.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

content.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
content.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ

content.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
content.summary: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

content.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
content.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.

content.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
content.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.

content.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
content.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total e

content.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
content.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.

content.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
content.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.

content.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
content.summary: For the week ending June 19, 2026, U.S. refineries processed 17.1 million barrels per day (b/d) of crude oil, down 81,000 b/d from the previous week, and they operated at 96.1% capacity utilization. Gasoline production averaged 9.5 million b/d, and distillate production increased to 5.2 million b/d.

content.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
content.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.

content.title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
content.summary: The Permian region's marketed natural gas production grew from 17.2 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2021 to 27.6 Bcf/d in 2025, a 60% increase, according to data from our latest Short Term Energy Outlook. Over the same period, crude oil production grew by 39%, going from 4.7 million barrels per day (b/d) to 6.6 million b/d. The higher growth in natural gas production is the result of increasing gas-oil ratios (GOR).

content.title: Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
content.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.

content.title: Largest wind farm in the United States slated to begin commercial operations
content.summary: The SunZia Wind Project, the largest wind farm in the United States, is slated to begin commercial operations this month. The wind farm, located in New Mexico, has a total net summer generating capacity of 3,650 megawatts (MW) and is composed of 916 wind turbines. SunZia's capacity is more than three times larger than the next two largest wind farms, Alta Wind in Southern California (1,098 MW) and Great Prairie in northern Texas (1,027 MW). The SunZia Wind Project works with a high voltage trans

content.title: L’energia in Parlamento questa settimana: 13-17 luglio 2026
content.summary:
Inizia una nuova settimana di lavori parlamentari su energia, clima ed efficienza. In Senato la Commissione Ambiente riprenderà l’esame della delega al Governo in materia di energia nucleare sostenibile e dei disegni di legge sulla cattura, il trasporto, l’utilizzo e lo stoccaggio della CO₂. Proseguirà inoltre l’esame del ddl per l’efficientamento dell’illuminazione pubblica e degli […]

content.title: Sicilia, agrivoltaico protagonista nelle procedure ambientali
content.summary:
Oltre 11,6 GW di impianti rinnovabili esaminati in sei mesi, con l’agrivoltaico che da solo rappresenta circa il 60% del totale. È uno dei dati principali del rapporto sull’attività svolta nel primo semestre 2026 dalla Commissione tecnica specialistica per le autorizzazioni ambientali della Regione Siciliana, organismo che formula i pareri nell’ambito delle procedure regionali di […]

content.title: Fotovoltaico preassemblato, quando il cantiere fa da fabbrica
content.summary:
Il fotovoltaico preassemblato, pieghevole o robotizzato sta diventando una risposta concreta al collo di bottiglia più stretto del settore: installare i moduli più rapidamente, con meno manodopera e meno imprevisti di cantiere. La riprova emerge da alcuni dati: nel 2025, le rinnovabili hanno aggiunto 692 GW nel mondo, di cui 511 GW fotovoltaici, secondo l’Agenzia […]

The post Fotov

content.title: Le modifiche al ddl aree idonee in Piemonte
content.summary:
Più spazio agli accumuli, al mini-eolico e al fotovoltaico flottante, nuove regole per biometano e cave e una revisione dei criteri applicabili ai terreni agricoli. Si amplia, rispetto alla proposta della Giunta, il testo del disegno di legge piemontese sulle aree idonee licenziato l’8 luglio dalle commissioni Ambiente e Agricoltura del Consiglio regionale. Il provvedimento, […]

content.title: Sull’impianto eolico “Phobos” l’Umbria prova a imitare il Lazio
content.summary:
L’Umbria tenta una nuova strada per bloccare il parco eolico Phobos, composto da sette turbine per 42 MW totali che Rwe vuole installare tra Castel Giorgio e Orvieto, in privincia di Terni. L’ultima novità in ordine di tempo è una mozione approvata in Consiglio regionale il 9 luglio che impegna la Giunta ad attivarsi formalmente […]

content.title: Le materie prime critiche e le debolezze della strategia europea
content.summary:
La strategia europea per rafforzare la sicurezza degli approvvigionamenti di materie prime critiche, come litio, cobalto e terre rare, rischia di entrare in un vicolo cieco, senza riuscire a ridurre la dipendenza dalle importazioni da paesi terzi, in particolare dalla Cina. È il messaggio centrale del policy briefing pubblicato il 9 luglio dal think tank […]

content.title: Pmg e rifiuti legnosi, Arera impugna la sentenza del Tar
content.summary:
Arera ha deciso di impugnare la sentenza con cui il Tar Lombardia ha annullato in parte la delibera 305/2024 sui prezzi minimi garantiti per gli impianti a biomassa. Secondo l’Autorità, la pronuncia si presta a essere censurata perché basata su “un’erronea interpretazione degli elementi di fatto e di diritto rilevanti”, si spiega nella delibera 251/2026 […]

content.title: Fotovoltaico di grande taglia: come prepararsi alle nuove competenze
content.summary:
La crescita del fotovoltaico e, più in generale, delle fonti rinnovabili sta trasformando in profondità il mercato del lavoro in campo energetico. L’evoluzione tecnologica, l’integrazione con sistemi di accumulo e la crescente complessità delle reti elettriche richiedono oggi competenze aggiornate, trasversali e operative. Colmare il divario tra domanda e offerta di professionalità qualificate è una […]

content.title: Oil Soars on Hormuz Blockade
content.summary: Oil surged after President Trump announced plans to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping and impose fees on cargo moving through the Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than 2 Decades
content.summary: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years.

content.title: UAE Tells OPEC Its Oil Production Surged Last Month
content.summary: The United Arab Emirates informed OPEC that its oil production surged by 80 percent last month.

content.summary: Or 44 percent full - depending on how you want to look at it.

content.title: South Africa Plans First Oil Reserves Boost Since Apartheid
content.summary: South Africa plans to increase its strategic oil reserves for the first time since the apartheid government stockpiled crude.

content.title: Falling Fertility Threatens Energy Demand, WoodMac Warns
content.summary: Falling fertility rates threaten long term energy demand, Wood Mackenzie warned.

content.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday.

content.title: TotalEnergies Sells Small-Scale Solar Assets in Europe
content.summary: The sale is part of TotalEnergies' efforts 'to refocus its renewable development on large utility-scale solar and wind farms in order to benefit from economies of scale', the French energy giant said.

content.title: Birol Urges EU to Reconsider Arctic Drilling Moratorium
content.summary: International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol pressed the European Union to revisit its moratorium on drilling for oil and gas in the Arctic.
